hero.headline: MAHE Online MBA in Finance: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #3 University 2025 | Institution of Eminence | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹2,92,000

sections.tldr: MAHE Online MBA in Finance covers security analysis and portfolio management, financial analytics, financial derivatives, fintech, fixed income securities, and corporate valuation. NAAC A++ NIRF #3 University with IoE tag. Total fee ₹2,92,000 (₹73,000/sem). Zero-cost EMI ₹12,167/month. Coursera Plus bundled free. 8 scholarship categories. Work experience required; fresh graduates submit SOP video.

about.body:
MAHE's Online MBA in Finance covers both traditional corporate finance and contemporary fintech. Semester 3 covers security analysis and portfolio management (equity valuation, MPT, CAPM, portfolio construction), financial analytics (data-driven financial decision-making, financial modelling basics), and financial derivatives (options, futures, swaps, hedging strategies). Semester 4 adds fintech (blockchain in finance, digital payments, robo-advisory, regtech), fixed income securities (bond valuation, yield curves, credit risk), and corporate valuation (DCF, comparable analysis, M&A valuation).

The inclusion of fintech as a standalone subject alongside traditional finance subjects is a curriculum strength. Most competing finance MBAs either focus entirely on traditional finance or on fintech, but MAHE covers both in a single programme.

MAHE holds NIRF #3 University 2025, NAAC A++, and Institution of Eminence status. Coursera Plus is bundled free. At ₹2,92,000, the fee is premium but the NIRF #3 credential carries weight with BFSI employers who filter on university rank.

Work experience in reputed organisations is required. Fresh graduates can apply with an SOP video.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Finance specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost analysis, budgeting, variance analysis, and management decision-making using accounting data.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Contract law, consumer protection, intellectual property, company law basics, and regulatory compliance.

subjects.name: Minor Project
subjects.desc: Applied finance research project supervised by faculty.

subjects.name: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
subjects.desc: Equity valuation methods (DCF, PE, PBV), Modern Portfolio Theory, CAPM, efficient frontier, portfolio construction, and rebalancing strategies.

subjects.name: Financial Analytics
subjects.desc: Data-driven financial decision-making, financial modelling basics, ratio analysis automation, and financial dashboard design.

subjects.name: Financial Derivatives
subjects.desc: Options pricing (Black-Scholes basics), futures contracts, swaps, hedging strategies, and derivative risk management.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship and Innovation
subjects.desc: Startup ideation, business model canvas, lean startup methodology, and funding strategies.

subjects.name: Sustainability
subjects.desc: Sustainable business models, ESG frameworks, and CSR strategy.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Comprehensive finance capstone integrating Sem 1-4 learning with industry-linked problem.

subjects.name: Fintech: Emerging Technologies in Finance
subjects.desc: Blockchain in finance, digital payments (UPI, CBDC), robo-advisory platforms, regtech, and AI-driven credit scoring.

subjects.name: Fixed Income Securities
subjects.desc: Bond valuation, yield curve analysis, duration and convexity, credit risk assessment, and fixed income portfolio management.

subjects.name: Corporate Valuation
subjects.desc: DCF valuation, comparable company analysis, precedent transactions, M&A valuation, and LBO model basics.

whoHires.body:
MAHE MBA Finance graduates target roles across banking, corporate finance, and investment management:

**Industries:** Commercial and investment banking, asset management, insurance, NBFC, corporate treasury, equity research, PE/VC (associate level), fintech, and financial consulting

**Entry-level roles:** Financial Analyst, Credit Analyst, Equity Research Associate, Investment Banking Analyst, Treasury Analyst, Risk Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Finance Manager, Portfolio Manager (junior), Credit Manager, Treasury Manager, Financial Controller, Fintech Product Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CFO, VP Finance, Head of Treasury, Director of Investments, Chief Risk Officer, Head of Corporate Finance

**Employers:** Banks (HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Kotak, Axis Bank, SBI), asset management (HDFC AMC, SBI MF, ICICI Prudential), consulting (McKinsey, BCG, Deloitte financial advisory), and fintech (Razorpay, PhonePe, Paytm).

**Note:** MAHE provides career support services. The Manipal alumni network is strong in healthcare, banking, and technology sectors.

skills.body:
The Finance specialisation builds skills across capital markets, corporate finance, and fintech:

**Technical skills:**
- Portfolio Management: MPT application, CAPM beta calculation, portfolio optimisation, risk-adjusted return metrics (Sharpe, Treynor, Jensen's alpha)
- Derivatives: Black-Scholes option pricing, delta hedging, interest rate swap valuation, and currency futures strategy
- Corporate Valuation: DCF model construction, WACC estimation, comparable analysis, and M&A premium analysis
- Fixed Income: bond price-yield relationship, duration matching, credit spread analysis, and yield curve interpretation

**Applied skills:**
- Financial Analytics: financial dashboard design, ratio analysis automation, and data-driven credit assessment
- Fintech: digital payment infrastructure understanding, robo-advisory platform evaluation, and regtech compliance framework

**Soft skills:** Presenting investment theses to committees, negotiating credit terms, managing regulatory relationships, and communicating financial risk to non-finance stakeholders.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MAHE Online MBA Finance against peer programmes:

**vs MUJ Online MBA Finance:** MUJ at approximately ₹1,80,000 on the same platform. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹1,12,000 more for the NIRF #3 and IoE brand. Both have similar LMS delivery. MAHE includes Coursera Plus. If your employer values the MAHE flagship brand specifically (not just Manipal group), the premium may be justified for BFSI roles where credential signalling matters.

**vs Amrita Online MBA Finance:** Amrita at ₹2,20,000 with NIRF #8 and NAAC A++. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹72,000 more but carries NIRF #3 (vs #8). MAHE includes Coursera Plus; Amrita does not. Both hold NAAC A++. MAHE wins on NIRF positioning.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA Finance:** NMIMS at approximately ₹1,96,000 has strong Mumbai BFSI recruiter connections and legacy brand in finance. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 costs ₹96,000 more with stronger NIRF rank. For Mumbai-centric BFSI careers, NMIMS brand carries specific weight.

**vs DPU Pune Online MBA Finance:** DPU at ₹1,89,400 with NIRF #41 and NAAC A++. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 is substantially more expensive but carries NIRF #3 and IoE. DPU includes edX; MAHE includes Coursera Plus.

fees.body:
The MAHE Online MBA in Finance has a total programme fee of ₹2,92,000 inclusive of all taxes.

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹73,000
- Semester 2: ₹73,000
- Semester 3: ₹73,000
- Semester 4: ₹73,000
- **Total: ₹2,92,000**

**Application fee:** ₹500 one-time.

**Zero-cost EMI:** ₹12,167/month directly through the university.

**Scholarships (8 categories):**
- Defence Personnel / Ex-servicemen: 20%
- Divyaang / PwD: 20%
- Merit: 10% (75%+ aggregate)
- MAHE/AGE Alumni: 15%
- SAARC and EWC Nationals: 40%
- Female Learners: 15%
- Doctors: 15%
- Corporate Employees: 15%

**Important:** Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers.

**Bundled:** Coursera Plus access free with the programme.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with MAHE each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Finance as the specialisation, issued by Manipal Academy of Higher Education (MAHE) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #3 University rank, Institution of Eminence status, and WES recognition. Valid for banking promotions, PSU finance roles, SEBI-regulated entity positions, and private-sector corporate finance recruitment. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from MAHE Online MBA Finance students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Arun K.
items.liked: Corporate Valuation and Financial Derivatives subjects were well-structured. Coursera Plus gave access to CFA prep content alongside the MBA
items.disliked: ₹2,92,000 is steep when MUJ offers a finance MBA on the same platform for ₹1,12,000 less. The MAHE brand premium should come with better placement infrastructure
items.body: I chose MAHE specifically for the NIRF #3 credential for my BFSI career. The finance subjects were rigorous. The fintech module covering blockchain, digital payments, and robo-advisory was a genuine differentiator. Coursera Plus supplemented nicely with Python for Finance courses. The cost is the main concern. Unless the MAHE flagship tag specifically matters for your employer, MUJ gives you nearly identical content delivery.

items.name: Priya D.
items.liked: Security Analysis and Portfolio Management was taught by faculty with industry experience. The fixed income module was deeper than what most online MBAs cover
items.disliked: Some pre-recorded lectures felt dated. The financial analytics module could include more hands-on Excel/Python modelling
items.body: I work in corporate treasury at a pharma exporter. The derivatives, international finance coverage in the core, and portfolio management subjects were directly applicable. NIRF #3 and IoE cleared every credential filter. The 15% corporate employee scholarship brought my effective fee down, which made the decision easier.

items.name: Deepak S.
items.liked: Fintech module was current and relevant. MAHE brand recognition in South India is genuinely strong for banking interviews
items.disliked: Career services could be more proactive. Got minimal direct placement support. Self-driven networking through the Manipal alumni community was more effective
items.body: The finance curriculum is comprehensive. The combination of traditional subjects (portfolio management, derivatives, fixed income) with fintech gives a balanced profile. My concern is that at ₹2,92,000, MAHE should offer more structured career support than what I received. The brand opens doors, but you still need to walk through them yourself.

faqs.question: Does the Finance MBA cover both traditional finance and fintech?
faqs.answer: Yes. Sem 3 covers traditional finance (portfolio management, derivatives, financial analytics). Sem 4 includes Fintech: Emerging Technologies in Finance alongside fixed income securities and corporate valuation. This dual coverage is a curriculum differentiator.

faqs.question: How does MAHE Finance compare to MUJ Finance?
faqs.answer: Same platform, similar delivery. MAHE at ₹2,92,000 carries NIRF #3 and IoE. MUJ at ₹1,80,000 costs ₹1,12,000 less. Choose MAHE if the flagship brand specifically matters. Choose MUJ if fee value is the priority.

faqs.question: Is Coursera Plus useful for finance careers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Coursera Plus gives free access to Google Data Analytics, Python for Finance, CFA prep content, and financial modelling courses from top universities. These supplement the MBA syllabus with practical tool skills.

faqs.question: Can loan applicants get scholarships?
faqs.answer: No. Learners opting for loan or financing are not eligible for additional discounts or offers. This is a critical consideration when comparing effective costs across programmes.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹2,92,000 total (₹73,000 per semester). ₹500 application fee. Zero-cost EMI at ₹12,167/month. 8 scholarship categories available (up to 40%). Reconfirm with our counsellor before payment.

chooseIf: NIRF #3 and Institution of Eminence credential signalling matters for BFSI employer filters.
You want traditional finance depth (derivatives, fixed income, portfolio management) combined with fintech in one programme.
Coursera Plus access for CFA prep and financial modelling supplements adds genuine value.
You qualify for a scholarship category that reduces the ₹2,92,000 effective fee.
consider: MUJ at ₹1,80,000 offers similar platform delivery for ₹1,12,000 less.
NMIMS at ₹1,96,000 has specific Mumbai BFSI recruiter networks.
Loan financing disqualifies all scholarships, making MAHE one of the most expensive online MBA options.
